pkg/pglockx.purpose: Thin wrapper around cirello.io/pglock that constructs a Postgres-backed distributed lock client over the `distributed_locks` table. Its primary constraint: the lock client is created via pglock.UnsafeNew and depends on validated lease/heartbeat timing.

patterns.name: Config.Validate before construction
patterns.description: New() calls config.Validate() first and refuses to build a client on invalid config, wrapping the error.
patterns.example: if err := config.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, fmt.Errorf("invalid lock configuration: %w", err) }

patterns.name: Lease/heartbeat invariant
patterns.description: Validate enforces LeaseTime must be at least twice HeartbeatInterval, and Owner must be non-empty; errors collected via errors.Join.
patterns.example: if c.LeaseTime/2 < c.HeartbeatInterval { errs = append(errs, errors.New(...)) }

key_file_guides.role: Defines Config (LeaseTime, HeartbeatInterval, Owner), Validate(), and New(db, config) returning *pglock.Client over the hardcoded `distributed_locks` table.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Uses pglock.UnsafeNew (not New) — table is fixed via WithCustomTable(lockTable). DefaultHeartbeatInterval=3s, DefaultLeaseTime=1m; preserve the lease>=2*heartbeat relationship if changing defaults.

anti_patterns: Calling pglock.New/UnsafeNew directly instead of going through New() and skipping config validation.
Setting HeartbeatInterval too close to LeaseTime (violates the 2x lease invariant).

decisions.decision: Use Postgres-based pglock with a custom `distributed_locks` table.
decisions.rationale: Reuses the existing Postgres dependency for distributed leader/worker locking instead of adding a separate coordination service.

pkg/pgxpoolobserver.purpose: OpenTelemetry instrumentation helper that registers observable metrics for a pgxpool.Pool. Single-function package consumed by pkg/framework/pgdriver to expose connection-pool stats.

patterns.name: Register-then-callback metric registration
patterns.description: Each metric is created via meter.Int64ObservableCounter/Gauge, appended to allMetrics, then a single RegisterCallback reads pool.Stat() and ObserveInt64s every value.
patterns.example: _, err = meter.RegisterCallback(func(_ context.Context, o metric.Observer) error { stat := pool.Stat(); o.ObserveInt64(acquireCountMetric, stat.AcquireCount(), ...); return nil }, allMetrics...)

patterns.name: Fail-fast on metric creation error
patterns.description: Every meter.Int64Observable* call is immediately error-checked and returns on failure before registering the callback.
patterns.example: acquireCountMetric, err := meter.Int64ObservableCounter("pgxpool.acquire_count", ...); if err != nil { return err }

patterns.name: Attribute pass-through
patterns.description: additionalAttributes variadic is forwarded to every ObserveInt64 via metric.WithAttributes so callers can tag pool metrics.

key_file_guides.role: Single exported func ObservePoolMetrics(meter, pool, ...attrs) that registers ~13 `pgxpool.*` metrics (acquire_count, idle_conns, total_conns, etc.) from pgxpool.Stat().
key_file_guides.watch_for: Avg acquire duration is only observed when acquireCount > 0 to avoid divide-by-zero. Implementation is adapted from cmackenzie1/pgxpool-prometheus; keep metric names `pgxpool.*` stable for dashboards.

anti_patterns: Registering multiple callbacks instead of one callback over allMetrics.
Computing avg acquire duration without guarding acquireCount > 0.

decisions.decision: One RegisterCallback reading pool.Stat() once per collection cycle.
decisions.rationale: pool.Stat() is a consistent snapshot; reading it once per observe cycle keeps all metrics coherent and cheap.

pkg/redis.purpose: Redis client factory and configuration for OpenMeter, supporting standalone and Sentinel failover modes with optional TLS, plus OTel tracing/metrics instrumentation.

patterns.name: Config struct with Validate + Configure(viper)
patterns.description: Config holds Address/Database/auth plus nested Sentinel and TLS structs; Validate() aggregates errors via errors.Join; Configure(v, prefix) sets viper defaults under a prefix.
patterns.example: func Configure(v *viper.Viper, prefix string) { v.SetDefault(fmt.Sprintf("%s.address", prefix), "127.0.0.1:6379") }

patterns.name: Functional options for instrumentation
patterns.description: NewClient(Options, ...Option) applies WithTracingProvider/WithMeterProvider options; nil providers are ignored.
patterns.example: func WithTracingProvider(p trace.TracerProvider) Option { return func(o *Options) { if p != nil { o.TracingProvider = p } } }

patterns.name: Sentinel vs standalone branch
patterns.description: If Sentinel.Enabled, build redis.NewFailoverClient with MasterName + SentinelAddrs=[Address]; otherwise redis.NewClient with Addr=Address.

patterns.name: Always instrument with redisotel
patterns.description: Both InstrumentTracing and InstrumentMetrics are always called (provider options only added when non-nil), errors wrapped with fmt.Errorf.
patterns.example: if err := redisotel.InstrumentTracing(client, tracingOpts...); err != nil { return nil, fmt.Errorf(...) }

key_file_guides.role: Options struct (embeds Config + providers), Option functions, and NewClient that builds + instruments the *redis.Client.
key_file_guides.watch_for: TLS forces MinVersion TLS1.3; InsecureSkipVerify is wired from config. Sentinel uses single Address as the sentinel addr list.

key_file_guides.role: Config struct, Validate(), Config.NewClient() convenience, and Configure(viper, prefix) defaults.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Configure sets an `expiration` default (24h) that is not a field on Config — it is read elsewhere. Validate requires Address and (when Sentinel enabled) MasterName.

anti_patterns: Constructing redis.Client directly instead of via NewClient/Config.NewClient (skips OTel instrumentation).
Adding config fields without a corresponding Configure() viper default and Validate() check.

decisions.decision: Embed Config inside Options and layer functional Options for providers.
decisions.rationale: Lets config-driven and DI-driven (tracer/meter) inputs combine without a wide constructor signature.

pkg/ref.purpose: Tiny value-type package providing IDOrKey — a reference that is either a ULID id or a string key — used widely across billing, ledger, and productcatalog to resolve resources by id-or-key.

patterns.name: ULID-detection parsing
patterns.description: ParseIDOrKey(s) tries ulid.Parse; success means it's an ID, failure means it's a Key.
patterns.example: if _, err := ulid.Parse(s); err != nil { n.Key = s } else { n.ID = s }

patterns.name: Validate requires one of id/key
patterns.description: IDOrKey.Validate() errors when both ID and Key are empty.
patterns.example: if i.ID == "" && i.Key == "" { return fmt.Errorf("either id or key is required") }

patterns.name: GetIDs/GetKeys nil-safe accessors
patterns.description: GetIDs/GetKeys return nil (not empty slice) when the respective field is empty, for easy spread into filter args.
patterns.example: func (i IDOrKey) GetIDs() []string { if i.ID == "" { return nil }; return []string{i.ID} }

key_file_guides.role: Defines IDOrKey{ID,Key} with json tags, Validate, GetIDs, GetKeys, and ParseIDOrKey.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Classification is purely ULID-shape based — a key that happens to be a valid ULID will be treated as an ID. Both fields can be set when constructed directly (e.g. from JSON), but ParseIDOrKey sets exactly one.

anti_patterns: Assuming a non-empty string is a key without trying ULID parse — use ParseIDOrKey.
Returning empty slices from GetIDs/GetKeys (breaks the nil-omit convention callers rely on).

pkg/server.purpose: Shared HTTP server middleware and request-logging primitives reused across both api/v3/server and openmeter/server, built on go-chi middleware and OTel semconv.

patterns.name: Middleware as func(http.Handler) http.Handler
patterns.description: Each middleware is a constructor returning the standard chi/net-http middleware signature; MiddlewareFunc aliases it for cross-version reuse.

patterns.name: OTel semconv attribute keys
patterns.description: Request attributes and log fields use go.opentelemetry.io/otel/semconv/v1.27.0 keys (HTTPRequestMethodKey, URLFullKey, HTTPResponseStatusCodeKey) rather than ad-hoc strings.

patterns.name: chi LogFormatter/LogEntry implementation
patterns.description: RequestLogger implements middleware.LogFormatter and RequestLoggerEntry implements middleware.LogEntry (compile-time asserted via var _ ...), logging via slog at Debug on Write and Error on Panic.

key_file_guides.role: GetRequestAttributes(r) builds an OTel-semconv attribute map for a request; adds req_id from middleware.GetReqID.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Requires middleware.RequestID to be installed upstream or req_id will be absent. There is no semconv key for request id, so a custom `req_id` key is used.

key_file_guides.role: NewRequestLoggerMiddleware(slog.Handler) plus RequestLogger/RequestLoggerEntry implementing chi's logging hooks.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Successful requests log at slog.LevelDebug — they are silent at higher log levels. Panics log stack + panic value at Error.

key_file_guides.role: NewPoweredByMiddleware sets the X-Powered-By: "OpenMeter by Kong, Inc." header on every response.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Header value is a constant; change it in one place. Covered by poweredby_test.go.

key_file_guides.role: Declares the shared MiddlewareFunc type used across API versions.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Keep this signature identical to net/http middleware so chi .Use accepts it directly.

anti_patterns: Using hardcoded attribute/log-field strings instead of semconv keys.
Logging successful requests above Debug level (breaks the intended quiet default).
Duplicating the X-Powered-By value instead of using the constant in poweredby.go.

decisions.decision: Centralize cross-version HTTP middleware here rather than in api/v3 or openmeter/server.
decisions.rationale: Both server stacks import pkg/server so request logging, attributes, and headers stay consistent across API versions.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a response-header middleware in chi style
